summary.en: A purpose adverbial clause says why the main clause happens. Use 'in order that' or 'so that' (never 'so that' at the very start), and the clause usually carries a modal like can, could, may, might, should or would.
summary.zh: 目的副词从句说明主句为何发生。用 in order that 或 so that 引导（so that 不能放句首），从句里一般带情态动词 can、could、may、might、should、would。

body.en: A purpose adverbial clause is easy to grasp and carries a cause–effect feel, but stated as a purpose: because you want to reach goal A (the clause), you do B (the main clause) — or equally, you do B in order to reach A.
body.zh: 目的副词从句很好理解，也带有一定的因果关系，只不过是用目的的方式说出来：因为要达到目的 A（从句），所以做了 B（主句）；或者说，之所以做 B（主句），是为了达到目的 A（从句）。

concepts.sourceRef: 因为要达到目的A，从句，所以做了B主句

heading.zh: in order that 与 so that

body.en: Both 'in order that' and 'so that' introduce a purpose clause and mean essentially the same thing. The one rule: 'so that' cannot stand at the very front of the sentence, while 'in order that' can.
body.zh: in order that 和 so that 都引导目的从句，意思基本相同。唯一的规则是：so that 不可以放在句首，而 in order that 可以。

concepts.sourceRef: 注意，so that 不可以放在句首。

heading.en: Use a modal inside the clause
heading.zh: 从句内一般用情态动词

body.en: Inside a purpose clause you normally use a modal verb — can, could, may, might, should, would — because a modal brings out the sense of intended purpose better than a plain verb.
body.zh: 目的副词从句中一般使用情态动词 can、could、may、might、should、would 等，因为这样能更好地体现目的性。

concepts.sourceRef: 目的副词从句中一般使用情态动词 can, could, may, might, should, would 等，因为这样能更好体现目的性

examples.text: In order that I could finish the video in time, I pulled an all-nighter.

annotations.quote: I could finish the video in time

note.en: 'In order that' can open the sentence. The goal (finishing in time) is stated first; the modal 'could' marks it as an intended purpose.
note.zh: in order that 可以放在句首。先说出目的（及时完成），情态动词 could 体现这是要达成的目的。

examples.text: I pulled an all-nighter so that I could finish the video in time.

annotations.quote: I could finish the video in time

note.en: Almost the same meaning as e1, but with 'so that' the main clause comes first — 'so that' must follow it, never lead.
note.zh: 意思与 e1 基本相同，但用 so that 时主句在前——so that 必须跟在主句后，不能领头。

examples.text: So that I could finish the video in time, I pulled an all-nighter.

note.en: Incorrect: 'so that' cannot stand at the very front of the sentence. Use 'in order that' here instead.
note.zh: 错误：so that 不可以放在句首。这里应改用 in order that。

prompt.en: Which marker is NOT allowed at the very start of a sentence?
prompt.zh: 哪个引导词不能放在句首？

text.en: both are fine at the start

explain.en: 'So that' cannot lead a sentence; it must follow the main clause. 'In order that' can open the sentence.
explain.zh: so that 不能领头，必须跟在主句之后；in order that 可以放句首。

prompt.en: Fill the blank with the modal from the transcript: 'I pulled an all-nighter so that I ____ finish the video in time.'
prompt.zh: 用讲解中的情态动词填空：'I pulled an all-nighter so that I ____ finish the video in time.'

explain.en: A purpose clause normally takes a modal; here the transcript uses 'could' to mark the intended purpose.
explain.zh: 目的从句一般用情态动词；讲解中用 could 体现这一目的。

quizzes.sentence: So that I could finish the video in time, I pulled an all-nighter.
quizzes.fix: In order that I could finish the video in time, I pulled an all-nighter.

explain.en: 'So that' cannot sit at the front of the sentence. When the purpose comes first, use 'in order that'.
explain.zh: so that 不能放在句首。目的从句放在前面时要用 in order that。

prompt.en: Why do purpose clauses usually contain a modal like 'could' or 'might'?
prompt.zh: 目的从句为什么一般含 could、might 这类情态动词？

text.en: Because a modal better brings out the sense of intended purpose
text.zh: 因为情态动词能更好体现目的性

text.en: Because the clause must always be in the past tense
text.zh: 因为从句必须用过去时

text.en: Because 'so that' grammatically requires a modal after it
text.zh: 因为 so that 后面语法上必须接情态动词

explain.en: The transcript's reason is meaning, not a hard rule: a modal (can, could, may, might, should, would) brings out the intended-purpose sense.
explain.zh: 讲解给的理由是为了表意，而非硬性规则：情态动词（can、could、may、might、should、would）能更好体现目的性。

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.text: Why do you look so tired today?

note.en: Sets up the cause/purpose to be explained.
note.zh: 引出要解释的原因/目的。

lines.speaker: Me
lines.text: I pulled an all-nighter so that I could finish the video in time.

annotations.quote: I could finish the video in time

note.en: Main clause first, then 'so that' + modal — the standard order for 'so that'.
note.zh: 主句在前，再用 so that + 情态动词——so that 的标准语序。

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.text: You could just say: In order that I could finish it in time, I stayed up.

annotations.quote: I could finish it in time

note.en: Same idea fronted with 'in order that', which is allowed at the start.
note.zh: 同样的意思用 in order that 提到句首——这是允许的。
